public.name: HDFC Nifty Smallcap 250 ETF
public.regime_lens: 🚦 RED — HELD but materially HEALING: the mechanically-forced silver floor breach that drove the 260628 downgrade has largely reversed — XAGUSD reclaimed ~+10% off the low to $62.985 (cache 7/3) / ~$62.3 (web), climbing all the way back to the $63 weekly EXIT-ALL gate (intraday high $63.35), with G:S compressing to ~66-67 from ~69 as silver OUT-performed gold; the rates pillar softened as a weak June NFP (+57K, prior two months revised −74K) faded the December-hike pricing from ~80% to ~66% and held TNX below the 4.45 gate (4.372); DXY eased off its one-year high to 100.857 (cushion back to ~1.14) and oil stayed sub-pre-war (Brent 72.13); RED is retained conservatively for the letter of the gates only — the weekly silver close is ~1.5¢ under the $63 EXIT-ALL line and the $67.87 F2 abort-all floor is still ~$5 unreclaimed — so this is a de-risk-and-watch RED converging back toward the operator's held YELLOW, not a deteriorating one; the lone adverse delta is a second RBI-reserve WATCH-breach print ($666.9B, ~$17B above the $650B PAUSE line) with USDINR softening to 95.2; new deployment stays OFF, but the surviving silver position is now vindicated by the bounce rather than an exit candidate, and a decisive weekly silver close above $63 re-opens the YELLOW / F109 conversation · base: Geopolitical Stagflation over Policy Transition, still a Warsh-era hawkish-LEANING regime but with the tightening impulse SOFTENING this week — the market has stepped back from an ~80%-priced December hike to ~66% after a weak June labor print (+57K, revisions −74K, participation falling), the July FOMC is priced ~76% hold, and TNX sits below the 4.45 gate; core inflation is still sticky (May core PCE 3.4%) so the hawkish lean is not gone, but the two regime layers have moved back toward offsetting rather than compounding tighter, and the dominant impulse is no longer clearly tightening — trajectory NEUTRAL · shock: Iran/Hormuz RESOLVED-to-disinflationary and UNCHANGED — the US-Iran deal is holding, the Strait of Hormuz is normalizing, WTI/Brent stayed sub-pre-war (Brent 72.13) with the war premium gone; the geopolitical shock remains a clean tailwind and the operative stress stayed ENDOGENOUS (the healing silver leg plus the RBI reserve burn), with a deal-collapse oil re-spike the only residual geopolitical tail and no re-escalation this week · action: RED = no new deployment anywhere; existing positions = manage/hold, not force-exit — the silver E... (refreshed 2026-07-05)
public.level_id: Range/basing · small-cap breadth gauge. Buy the 165–172 support band; weekly close >187 → 190.8 / 198.8 / 205.6; invalidation = weekly close <157. LTP 178.6 at the range-high cap (178–187).
